supabase/supabase#42176.body:
# Bug report

<!-- 

⚠️ We receive a lot of bug reports which have already been solved or discussed. If you are looking for help, please try these first:

- Docs: https://docs.supabase.com
- Discussions: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions
- Discord: https://discord.supabase.com

Before opening a bug report, please verify the following: 

-->

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

### Describe the bug

  The Cron integration dashboard in Supabase Studio fails to parse cron schedules that use pg_cron's `$` syntax (representing "last day of month"). Jobs with schedules like `0 0 $ * *` display "Unable to parse next run for job" in the "Next run" column, even though these are valid pg_cron schedules that execute correctly.

### To Reproduce

1. Go to **Integrations > Cron > Jobs** in Studio
2. Create a cron job with schedule `0 0 $ * *` (or any schedule using `$`)
3. Observe the "Next run" column shows "Unable to parse next run for job"

### Expected behavior

The dashboard should:
1. Recognize `$` as valid pg_cron syntax for "last day of month"
2. Calculate and display the correct next run time

### Screenshots

![pg_cron $ syntax not supported](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0b20b21e-5d16-4235-a4a4-59c00d7080f6)

*Jobs with `$` in schedule show "Unable to parse next run for job" while standard cron syntax works correctly*

### System information

- **OS:** macOS
- **Browser:** Chrome
- **Studio version:** Latest (supabase.com/dashboard)

### Additional context

The `$` syntax is a pg_cron-specific extension that represents "last day of the month". It's documented in [pg_cron](https://github.com/citusdata/pg_cron#cron-syntax).

**Root cause:** The frontend uses `cron-parser` library which supports `L` for "last day of month", but pg_cron uses `$` instead. The `$` character causes parsing to fail.

**Proposed fix:** Normalize pg_cron's `$` syntax to `cron-parser`'s `L` syntax before parsing. Both represent "last day of month" - it's just a syntax difference between the two systems.
supabase/supabase#42176.title: [Studio] pg_cron dashboard doesn't support `$` (last day of month) syntax in schedule parsing

supabase/supabase#43258.body:
# Bug report

<!-- 

⚠️ We receive a lot of bug reports which have already been solved or discussed. If you are looking for help, please try these first:

- Docs: https://docs.supabase.com
- Discussions: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions
- Discord: https://discord.supabase.com

Before opening a bug report, please verify the following: 

-->

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

It's not currently possible to import a CSV file in Supabase Studio with a genuine empty string value, which is not the same as a NULL value. It treats empty cells as null, but it also treats NULL as null, so there's two ways to enter a null value and no way to enter an empty string.

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

1. Go to Supabase Studio.
2. Open the table editor with some table.
3. Insert -> import CSV and import a file with some empty cells.
4. See that the imported data has empty cells set to NULL (even in the preview).

## Expected behavior

It should treat empty cells as empty strings or at least treat EMPTY as empty strings (which is how they're displayed in the table editor). But in the latter case you'd have to have a way to escape that to actually enter the value "EMPTY".
supabase/supabase#43258.title: It's not possible to import CSV with empty string value

supabase/supabase#44024.body:
## Describe the bug

Studio's table editor can generate invalid SQL when using `Copy as SQL` or `Export as SQL`.

I hit this on `storage.buckets`, where the generated output included values like:

```sql
'true'
'false'
'10485760'
ARRAY["image/*"]
```

Those are not correct PostgreSQL literals for this case.

## Expected behavior

The generated SQL should emit proper Postgres literals, e.g.:

```sql
true
false
10485760
ARRAY['image/*']
```

## Actual behavior

The SQL exporter currently wraps booleans and numbers as string literals, and emits text array elements with double quotes inside `ARRAY[...]`.

## Reproduction

1. Open Studio table editor
2. Pick a table with boolean, numeric, or `text[]` columns
3. Select one or more rows
4. Use `Copy as SQL` or `Export as SQL`
5. Run the generated SQL in Postgres

## Likely source

This appears to come from the shared formatter in:

- `apps/studio/components/interfaces/TableGridEditor/TableEntity.utils.ts`

That formatter is used by both row copy and full row export paths.

## Notes

A small fix here would be to:
- emit booleans and numbers as raw literals
- emit `text[]` members as SQL string literals inside `ARRAY[...]`
supabase/supabase#44024.title: Studio "Copy as SQL" / "Export as SQL" emits invalid PostgreSQL literals

supabase/supabase#44099.body:
I am experiencing an issue with webhooks in Supabase. I had an existing (old) webhook configured in my project. When I try to edit the webhook and change only the `URL`; I click **Save** and Supabase shows that the webhook is saved successfully. However, when I open the webhook again, the `URL` has reverted to a different/old value instead of the one I just saved.

This caused issues in production because the webhook was still sending requests to the old `URL` even though the dashboard showed that my `URL` change was saved.

### To Reproduce
1. Go to Supabase Dashboard → Database → Webhooks
2. Open an existing webhook
3. Change the webhook `URL`
4. Click Save
5. Reopen the webhook

The `URL` is different from the one that was saved (reverted to old `URL`)

### Expected behavior
The webhook `URL` should remain the same as the one entered after clicking Save.

### Actual behavior
The webhook `URL` changes back to a previous/incorrect `URL` after saving.

### Workaround
Deleting the webhook and creating a new one with the correct `URL` works. The issue only happens when editing an existing webhook.

Please let me know if you need any logs or additional information.
supabase/supabase#44099.title: Webhook URL not saving correctly – reverts to incomplete URL after saving

supabase/supabase#44146.body:
# 🐛 Bug report

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application.  
- [x] I confirm I have searched the Docs, GitHub Discussions, and Discord.  

## Problem

The cron job creation form in the Database Functions section has UI inconsistencies.

Specifically, scrolling does not behave correctly within the form (like the index/create form earlier), 
making it difficult to access all fields when the content overflows the viewport.

## To Reproduce

1. Open the Supabase Dashboard  
2. Navigate to Integration
3. Create a new cron job  
4. Attempt to scroll within the form  

## Expected behavior

The form should allow smooth scrolling similar to other form, ensuring all fields are accessible.

## Screenshots

N/A

## System information

- OS: Tested on multiple environments  
- Browser: Chrome / others  

## Additional context
Same like the index form
supabase/supabase#44146.title: new cron job form issue

supabase/supabase#44375.body:
## Bug report

### Describe the bug

Several files in `apps/studio/data/database-queues/` construct SQL queries by directly interpolating user-controlled values into template literals instead of using parameterized queries. While `queueName` is validated via regex (`isQueueNameValid`), other values like `payload` in the send mutation and `afterTimestamp` in the pagination query are interpolated without sanitization.

### Affected files

- `database-queue-messages-infinite-query.ts` — `afterTimestamp` is interpolated directly:
  ```ts
  query += ` WHERE enqueued_at > '${afterTimestamp}'`
  ```
- `database-queue-messages-send-mutation.ts` — `payload` is interpolated directly:
  ```ts
  sql: `select * from pgmq.send( '${queueName}', '${payload}', ${delay})`
  ```
- `database-queue-messages-delete-mutation.ts` — `messageId` (number type, lower risk but still not parameterized)
- `database-queue-messages-archive-mutation.ts` — same pattern with `messageId`

### Impact

The `payload` field in the send mutation is the most concerning since it accepts arbitrary user-provided JSON content. A payload containing a single quote (`'`) will at minimum break the query; a crafted payload could potentially execute arbitrary SQL. The `afterTimestamp` comes from `enqueued_at` values returned by previous queries, which is lower risk but still not safe to interpolate.

Even though `executeSql` runs through the project's SQL execution pipeline (which may use a service role connection), the defense-in-depth principle still applies — these should use parameterized queries or at minimum escape the interpolated values.

### Expected behavior

SQL parameters should be passed through parameterized query mechanisms rather than string interpolation. For the `pgmq` function calls, this could look like:
```ts
sql: `SELECT * FROM pgmq.send($1, $2::jsonb, $3)`,
params: [queueName, payload, delay]
```

Or at minimum, the values should be escaped before interpolation using a function like `pg-meta`'s `ident()` or a dedicated escape utility.

### System information

- Affected area: Studio Dashboard — Queues feature
- All files under: `apps/studio/data/database-queues/`
supabase/supabase#44375.title: Dashboard: Queue Messages queries use string interpolation for SQL parameters

vercel/ai#10060.title: OpenAI optional setting to not pass through `itemId`s in `providerOptions`

### Link to the code that reproduces this issue

https://github.com/Dan6erbond/revline/blob/a608ed5646019682456d65a42bd4abc3562d224e/client/src/components/performance/drag-sessions/create.tsx#L48

### To Reproduce

1. Set a `BASE_PATH` in `.env` like `/app`
2. Build and run the app
3. Create a car & drag session

### Current vs. Expected behavior

The app will redirect to `/app/app/cars/...` instead of `/app/cars/...`

### Provide environment information
